We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly enforce the posting of

of Final Acceptance by the agency. To ensure that the warranty security is
posted, the LGU may make it a requirement to observe the one (1) year
period after completion of the project before returning the retention money
after which the warranty period shall commence. Likewise, observe the
period requirement provided in Section 62.2.3.2 in the imposition of the
warranty security which is 2 to 15 years after final acceptance depending on the nature of the infrastructure project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:

ensure that the PERT/CPM should include all the holidays and other
instances that could adversely affect the construction works;
(e) Municipal Engineer to validate the facts and extent of delay before
recommending the grant of work suspension/extension of contract time;
and
(f) Municipal Engineer to regularly monitor/conduct ocular inspection
during the construction period to appropriately assess the performance
of the contractors in all the infrastructure works of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
(c) The BAC and the Technical Working Group to submit a written
justification on the lapses noted during the procurement process and
consider the following information submitted by the interested bidders
on its ensuing biddings:

disclosed in the Technical Proposals;
 List of equipment units owned/leased by the contractor; and
 The bidder is not blacklisted in the PhilGEPS website.
(d) The Municipal Engineer to intensify the monitoring of the
implementation of projects to immediately identify the contractors
which are in delay in the performance of its construction works and
recommend measures to avoid unjustified delays and other
unsatisfactory performance of the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor install control and monitoring mechanisms to ensure that the use of government vehicles and consumption of fuel shall strictly be for a valid public purpose. Henceforth, the following documents and reports shall be required on the use of government vehicles and fuel consumption: a. Serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets approved by the Municipal Mayor or his authorized representative to control the use of government vehicle; b. Monthly Report of Official Travels prepared by the driver concerned and Monthly Equipment Utilization Report prepared by the equipment custodian summarizing in chronological order his trips or use of equipment, respectively, for the month, duly certified by the driver and approved by the Administrative Officer; c. Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and submitted monthly to the auditor for verification purposes. The report shall show the different types of motor vehicles utilized by the agency during the month, total distance traveled, total fuel consumed and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle; and d. Submit to the Audit Team the “Bill For” used in the purchase of fuel, oil and lubricants for the period CY 2019.

The Municipal Accountant to issue Order of Payment for Liquidated Damages as determined and recommended by the Engineer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person/committee to attend trainings to enhance their knowledge on gender audit and analysis in order to produce gender responsive GPB, identify gender issues within the mandate of the LGU and include in the GPB the causes of the gender issues, assess the impact of GAD activities and policies in relation to gender equality of the constituents and employees and monitor the GPB implementation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the responsible officers to ensure the following:
The Municipal Engineer to intensify the monitoring of all on-going projects to avoid unjustified delays in compliance with the approved contract and to impose appropriate actions and remedies to erring contractors;
Forfeit performance security in accordance with the provisions of the contract; and
Take over the contract or award the contract to a qualified contractor through negotiation; and
The Municipal Accountant to issue Order of Payment for Liquidated Damages as determined and recommended by the Engineer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Accountant and Treasurer to comply with the preparation and submission of the Fund Utilization Report.  Also, we recommended to immediately return unused/excess fund of ₱1,333,716.56.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create Appraisal Committee, require the appraisal of the said PPE and facilitate the immediate disposal of the same to prevent further deterioration and loss of value pursuant to Section 79 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that management prepare the Capacity Development Agenda and the Local Chief Executive should ensure that seminars and conventions attended is responsive to the organizational result and development outcome, keeping in mind the prudent use of the scarce financial resources of the LGU as required in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-139.
This audit recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance to unnecessary and excessive claims of traveling expenses.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to take up the cancellation of stale checks and record the transaction as Accounts Payable, while, the check which is outstanding for more than 2 years should be treated as surplus adjustment of the fund as against which it was previously charged.  Henceforth, require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the encashment of checks issued and send a written notice to the payee at least one (1) month before a check becomes stale informing hin/her the existence of check.
